description_long: This is a traditional pick hammer featuring a long, straight wooden handle and a robust, double-sided metal head. The head is composed of two pointed, slightly curved ends, ideal for breaking or prying hard surfaces such as concrete or stone. The wooden handle has a natural grain texture and a cylindrical shape, providing a comfortable grip for manual use. The metal head is dark, likely forged steel, and is securely mounted atop the handle with a visible collar. There are no visible markings, logos, or decorative elements on the tool. The hammer appears clean and well-maintained, with no signs of wear or damage.
description: A classic pick hammer with a wooden handle and a double-pointed, curved metal head, designed for breaking or prying hard surfaces.
description_view_0: The hammer is shown from the side, clearly displaying the full length of the wooden handle and both pointed, curved ends of the metal head.
description_view_1: The hammer is viewed from the top, showing the circular end of the handle and the narrow profile of the metal head.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the same features as view 0, with the handle and both points of the head visible.
description_view_3: The hammer is viewed from the bottom, highlighting the end of the handle and the thin edge of the metal head.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with a natural grain texture, cylindrical and smooth, no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: double-pointed, curved pick head made of steel, with both ends tapering to a point for breaking or prying
